rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NORMA MOGANNAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/14/2023, as the following issue e-mailed to counsel on 11/15/2022 remains unaddressed: According to the schedule of liabilities attached to the amendment to the third account (filed on 03/02/2020), the total amount of the conservator's outstanding fees was $25,549.39; and the total amount of the conservator's attorneys' outstanding fees/costs was $8,245.76. According to the schedule of disbursements attached to this fourth account (Schedule C, Page 5), the estate paid $8,000 to the attorneys and $18,890 to the conservator toward their outstanding fees. However, the liabilities set forth in the amendment to the fourth account (filed on 06/23/2022) did not reduce the conservator's outstanding fees by $18,890 and attorneys' outstanding fees by $8,000. The conservator's outstanding fees should be $6,659.39 (= $25,549.39 - $18,890) + $59,160 (awarded by 07/17/2020 order on third account). The attorneys' outstanding fees/costs should be $245.76 (= $8,245.76 - $8,000) + $22,610.42 (awarded by 07/17/2020 order on third account). A corrected schedule of liabilities must be filed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LEONISA A. TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/28/23. (No earlier availability.) The beginning bank statement is not for the period immediately PRECEDING the date the conservators were appointed. [Probate Code Sect. 2620(c)(2).] It is also for the conservatorship's US Bank account, not the conservatee's. No statement has been filed to verify the ending balance. The Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575.] A final accounting must end on the date of death and also include a supplemental accounting following the date of death. What were the "additional assets received" from the Wells Fargo account? No Wells Fargo account was inventoried. There are also "miscellaneous" deposits on 10/19/21 and 12/16/21 without any source. The attorney costs of $1,745.84 paid on 6/10/21 still have not been itemized. There were credit card payments but no reported liabilities. Did the conservators continue to use the conservatee's credit cards? Statements may be required to confirm the purchases and amounts paid. There were life insurance premiums paid but no life insurance policy inventoried or on hand. The Court Investigator reported that the conservatee purchased a double burial plot in 2003, but none was inventoried or accounted for. The accounting for the period 7/15/21 - 9/14/22 shows a disbursements total on the summary that differs from the total shown on Schedule C-1. There is also a "misc. distribution" of $143 on 8/8/22. The prayer refers to the first account filed on 10/8/21 - which was taken off calendar - and an amended first and final account. This is the only first and final account before the Court. The petition does not allege that Michael Penfield qualifies to bill as a paralegal. This Court does not ordinarily allow legal fees for internal conferences or researching the Probate Code. Hearings on 6/22/22 and 10/12/22 were a result of failure to address issues with the account that had previously been filed. Courtesy copies of anything filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/16/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALICE M. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/28/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Additionally, although file stamped on 1/4/23, the amended petition was not actually e-filed until 1/6/23, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. Notice of the 2/28/23 hearing of the amended pleadings must be given at least 15 days before 2/28/23. (Why is the proposed schedule of distribution filed separately from the petition?) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has PREVIOUSLY BEEN CONFINED in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Not one of the I&As appears to have been verified by the personal representative. The Corrected Final I&A, verified by the attorney was reportedly verified on the same date as the one filed 4/26/22 (not 4/26/21, as still alleged in paragraph 7 of the amended petition), although it is reportedly "corrected". (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS APPOINTED.) The attorney was paid $3,824.25 for "legal" out of the sale proceeds. For what services or costs? Under what authority? The administrator has not yet addressed her holding estate funds in a bank outside of California. Now, it appears that funds are being held in the attorney's client trust account not bearing interest. The $50 the administrator deposited into the estate account to open the account is not a receipt of the estate includable in the fee base. (She was already reimbursed.) The attorney's declaration filed on 1/4/23 refers to original bank statements filed by the administrator on 1/2/23; no original bank statements were filed on 1/2/23. An amendment to amended petition must be filed to address these issues. Courtesy copies of the amendment to the amended petition and proof of service (and a revised proposed order, if applicable) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/16/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL SCHNORR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Notice was mailed to two of the heirs in NY at addresses other than those set forth in the petition. Either the petition must be supplemented with the correct/current addresses or notice must be given to those heirs at their address alleged in the petition (or waived by them). A courtesy copy of the supplement or correct proof of service (or waivers of notice) must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/8/23. NB: No courtesy copy of the proof of service filed on 8/31/22 was received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed. It is also noted that the administrator requested 60 days from the date of filing of this petition, which was 8/22/22 and would have meant October 2022, but no petition for final distribution has been filed yet. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF CLIFF LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No receipt for funds placed in a blocked account (MC-356 form) has been filed. The inventory and appraisal listed $29,125.87 as "anticipated annual income generated from the limited liability company known as the CLEE Properties, LLC". However, the guardian filed a declaration stating that the guardian has not received, and does not anticipate receiving in the near future, any cash distribution from the LLC. 2) No proof of recording of letters has been filed for the 6th Ave property. 3) The guardianship estate's 1/8 interest in the LLC is technically personal property that must be covered by a bond under Probate Code section 2320(c)(1). If a bond should be required, the total amount would be $758,000 (including 10-12% statutory recover costs). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MERVYN WYNN MEMORIAL TRAVEL FUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Fourteenth Account And Report Of De La Salle Institute And Petition For Settlement Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/28/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of service is incomplete item 2. Where in the accounting is the $25,940.33 earned income shown? Paragraph 4 of the 13th accounting indicated that $11,275.00 would be used to extend benefits to 23 students. This account and petition show $9,744.00 used for student travel grants for the 13th account period. How was the starting value determined to be $113,252.02 less than shown in the prior accounting ($104,130.80, not $217,382.82)? Exhibit T does not support this change; it is just this accounting with the same narrative explanation regarding how, as of December 2019, Commonfund started calculating the NAV differently, lumping together earned income, expenses, and changes in market value to reflect net assets of the fund divided by the number of shares owned by investors. Exhibit U does not support this either; it is just the statements showing the market values. Where is the support that this fund's 18,036.192 shares as of 4/30/19 had a book value of just $104,130.80? Attorney time sheets must be filed to support the request for $19,593.38 in fees. Is it not a conflict that a partner at the law firm representing the transferee empowered to distribute funds is married to the principal of the donee school? An appearance will likely be required for this issue after other issues with the accounting are addressed. (It was not necessary to attach copies of all previously filed - and already adjudicated - accountings and their associated orders or a copy of the will or order appointing the executor to this petition. The only operative document is the order that established the testamentary trust (or, in this instance, the travel fund) and any other order that modified the terms of that order for final distribution. For interim accountings, only the original statement verifying the ending balance is required to be filed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/16/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MATTHEW AND MELISSA WHITE 2007 LIFE INSURANCE TRUST AGREEMENT DATED NOVEMBER 7, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Modifying Trust To Change Beneficiaries Based On Consent Of Trustors And Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/07/2023 for further information: (1) Allegation why this petition is necessary. Petition merely states the dissolution judgment is silent about the premium payment, which appears insufficient to find the relief sought will or will not substantially impair the accomplishment of the purposes of the trust should the court grant it. (2) Clarification whether Petition seeks modification or termination. The caption asks modification; however, petition prays a paragraph. Petition to set forth which specific provision(s) need to be modified, for example, by striking the existing provisions and then by addiing how the provision(s) should be read. (3) Petition to Identify the trustee of each settlor's 2007 insurance trust. If applicable, said trustee(s) to be noticed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MON QUAN CHOW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/7/2023. Petitioner's allegation if this proceeding is necessary for the value of the estate. If needed, the following document must be filed two weeks prior. [1]. Duties and Liabilities of Personal Rep [DE-147]. [2] Confidential Statement of Birth Date and DL Number [DE-147S] pursuant to the LRSF 14.18.E. [3] Proof of Publication. PC 8120. [4] Notice of Remote Appearance. LRSF 14.1.D (6).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MANUEL R. PALERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Only a courtesy copy of the petition and a proposed order were received by the assigned examiner as of 1/10/23. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Eduardo R. Palero resides in Australia. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
